title: Package Distribution Contract
objective: Define how Quiver ships as a versioned package so the installer can consume stable assets instead of a manual folder copy.
description: Today Quiver is consumed by copying the repo folder as `docs-template/`. That is not a distribution contract. This slice defines the package layout, the shipped assets, and the repo-only files that must be excluded so the installer can rely on a stable payload.
must: Create the root package metadata needed to build and version a distributable Quiver package
Define which template assets are shipped to consumers and which repo-only files stay out of the package
Add a pack or artifact smoke check so maintainers can verify the package contents before publishing
acceptance: Maintainers can build a Quiver package artifact that contains only the intended template and runtime assets
The packaged asset layout is documented and stable enough for the future CLI to consume
Repo-only files such as `.git/` data and local development noise are excluded from the published payload
assumptions: The installer surface will be Node-based
The published package should remain consumable without cloning the full git repo
